message:
🌱 Add cloudevents: Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding mission (#1473)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cloudevents-177-extension-names-become-case-insensitive-in-binary-encoding",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "cloudevents: Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding",
+    "description": "Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current cloudevents setup",
+        "description": "Verify your cloudevents version:\nCloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)\nThis feature requires cloudevents as a dependency."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review cloudevents configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:\nExtensions are not defined to have case-insensitive names, but the binary encoding puts the extension name in a header. Since header names are case insensitive, their case may be changed by middleware. This makes extensions fairly fragile."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding",
+        "description": "A single header would be ok as long as the extensions are actually modeled as sub-properties of something. E.g. if in JSON they were:\n```\n\"extensions\": {\n  \"foo\": \"bar\"\n}\n```\nIf we allowed extensions to be top-level json properties then they're more akin to siblings of spec-approved attributes and\n```yaml\nCE-X: <key>: <value>\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A single header would be ok as long as the extensions are actually modeled as sub-properties of something. E.g. if in JSON they were:\n```\n\"extensions\": {\n  \"foo\": \"bar\"\n}\n```\nIf we allowed extensions to be top-level json properties then they're more akin to siblings of spec-approved attributes and I would prefer for them to be individual http headers.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "CE-X: <key>: <value>",
+        "\"extensions\": {\n  \"foo\": \"bar\"\n}",
+        "HeaderFoo: Bar\nHeaderFoo: Baz"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+      "cloudevents",
+      "graduated",

+      "feature"
+    ],

+    },
